content.subtitle: Use
items: To show the results of a situation or action: The hiker was so exhausted that he couldn't take another step. Sarah sang so beautifully that the audience gave her a standing ovation. There was so much traffic on the road that the coach couldn't reach the stadium in time.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: The word 'that' is not usually necessary to introduce the second clause.

examples.correct: The weather is so unpredictable people always carry an umbrella.
The word 'so' has a number of other uses. Try not to get confused between them.
I noticed the dark clouds, so I decided to take a waterproof jacket.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To show the results of a situation or action: It was such a spectacular match that the crowd cheered for twenty minutes. The travel guide had such detailed maps that we never got lost. There is such a lot of traffic on this route that the journey takes twice as long.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To show the results of a situation or action where there is/isn't the right amount/number of something: There aren't enough seats in the stadium for all the fans. It wasn't warm enough for the outdoor concert to go ahead. Did the team train hard enough to win?

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: A common mistake is to put 'enough' before an adjective when the correct word is 'quite' or 'fairly'.

examples.correct: It's quite / fairly cold in the mountains so make sure you bring a jacket.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To describe something that is more than necessary and which has a negative effect: The young musician was too nervous to perform on stage. We arrived too late for the beginning of the show. We spend too much money on takeaway food.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: We do not use 'too' when we want to describe something we consider to be positive. Instead, we use 'very', 'really' or 'extremely'.

examples.correct: You were very / really / extremely talented to win that competition.

sections.title: A: Comparative adjectives
sections.instructions: Complete using the comparative form of the adjective in brackets.

items.sentence: Your brother is much ...... (TALL) than mine.
This island used to be much ...... (GREEN) before the forest fires.
Slow down! You're ...... (FIT) than me and I can't keep up!
Veronica seems ...... (HAPPY) since she moved schools.
It's actually ...... (TRENDY) to wear your hair up this year.
As the time for the performance got nearer, I got ...... (NERVOUS).
Old people are often ...... (WISE) than young people.
Can you recommend a ...... (CHEAP) restaurant in this part of town?
Matt seems to have got even ...... (LAZY) than he used to be and almost never studies.
If the problem gets any ...... (SERIOUS), we may need to inform the manager about it.
If you work ...... (QUICKLY), you'll finish sooner and then you can go home earlier.
Ben says he's feeling much ...... (WELL) after his illness.
I have even ...... (LITTLE) free time this year than I did last year.
The news was much ...... (BAD) than anyone had feared.
You'll need to be able to run ...... (FAR) than this if you're going to do the marathon.

sections.title: B: Superlative forms
sections.instructions: Complete using the superlative forms of the words in capitals.

items.sentence: I think Mohammed Ali was the ...... (GREAT) boxer that ever lived.
It was the ...... (BORING) film I had ever seen.
Our teacher told us that the student with the ...... (HIGH) mark would get a prize.
Ginger is one of the ...... (LUCKY) cats alive - he's been hit by a car three times and has never been hurt!
Out of all the students in my music school, I practise ...... (OFTEN).
The Pacific is the ...... (DEEP) ocean in the world.
That's the ...... (UGLY) fish I've ever seen!
I was ill before the exam and I did ...... (BADLY) out of the whole class.
The ...... (FAR) I've run in one day is about ten kilometres.
Don't you think this would be the ...... (LOVELY) spot for a picnic?
But that's the ...... (CRAZY) idea I've ever heard!
I asked the assistant to show me the ...... (MODERN) phone they had.
I told the manager that it was the ...... (BAD) hotel I had ever stayed in.
Who do you think is the ...... (LITTLE) talented actor to win an Oscar?
Out of all the members of the choir, Jason sang ...... (WELL).

sections.title: C: Album review (gap fill)
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the word in brackets.

items.sentence: Silent Storm, the (1) **later / latest** album from rock band The Midnight Riders...
has to be one of the (2) **little / least** interesting releases I have ever heard.
The production is dreadful and the songwriting is much (3) **worse / worst** than The Midnight Riders' previous disappointment, Thunderbolt.
Silent Storm was apparently (4) **more / most** expensive to record than any other album this year...
The band claim it features the (5) **more / most** revolutionary guitar sound in modern music.
In practice, these musicians are far (6) **less / least** adventurous than they should be.
When I reviewed it, even (7) **younger / youngest** listeners who usually enjoy this style found it dull.
Jake Samson, the lead vocalist, does a slightly (8) **better / best** job than the other band members...
as concert ticket prices get (9) **higher / highest**, many fans are finding that the (10) **well / best** form of entertainment is to stay at home and stream music online.

sections.title: D: Key word transformation (comparison)
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
I have never read a better book than this one. **ever**
This is the ...... read.

Nobody has ever been this far into the jungle. **the**
This is ...... has ever been into the jungle.

I have never worked so hard in my whole life. **ever**
It was the ...... in my whole life.

Liam is the tallest boy in the class. **than**
Every other boy in the class ...... Liam.

This is the nicest beach along this part of the coast. **than**
This ...... the others along this part of the coast.

I read that Mount Everest is the highest mountain. **no**
I read that ...... Mount Everest.

Has anyone ever been this far north before? **the**
Is ...... has ever been?

The painting Ed did is the ugliest one you can imagine. **than**
You can't imagine ...... the one Ed did.
items.answer: an uglier painting/a painting uglier than

items.sentence:
This stamp is rarer than any other in my collection. **more**
The other stamps in my collection ...... this one.

Nobody in the class runs as fast as Pedro. **runner**
Pedro ...... in the class.
John can see over the wall because he is tall.
items.answer: John is so tall that he can see over the wall.

items.sentence: I'm sure my sister will go to university because she is clever.
items.answer: My sister is so clever that I'm sure she will go to university.

items.sentence: I can't stop playing this computer game because it's good.
items.answer: This computer game is so good that I can't stop playing it.

items.sentence: Tim can't come out because he has a lot of work to do.
items.answer: Tim has so much work to do that he can't come out.

items.sentence: It's hot, which means I can't sleep.
items.answer: It's so hot that I can't sleep.

items.sentence: Tina arrived late, which meant she missed the train.
items.answer: Tina arrived so late that she missed the train.

items.sentence: We don't have any money for luxuries because we have a lot of bills to pay.
items.answer: We have so many bills to pay that we don't have any money for luxuries.

items.sentence: It takes a day to get to Australia because it's very far away.
items.answer: Australia is so far away that it takes a day to get there.

items.sentence: Last summer, the heatwave arrived so **D** suddenly that many people had no time to prepare.
Last summer, there were so **A** many tourists in town that the roads were constantly blocked.
Last summer, we had such **C** a terrible drought that the rivers almost dried up.
Last summer, there was so **E** much sunshine that I got a tan in just two days.
Last summer, there was such a lot of **F** rain in August that the farmers were relieved.
Last summer was so **B** hot that the pavements were almost too warm to walk on.

sections.title: G: Enough
sections.instructions: There is an error in each sentence. The incorrect word or phrase is shown in bold. Write the correction on the line.

items.sentence: I don't think there's ...... (pizza enough) for everyone.
I'm afraid you're not ...... (enough old) to see this film.
Luckily, we got to the box office ...... (enough early) and got the tickets.
Anne's ...... (enough sensible) to realise she needs to work hard this year.
If you don't train ...... (enough hard), you'll never win the race.
Have you got ...... (credits enough) to call Yiota on your mobile?
It should be ...... (enough warm) for a picnic this weekend.
I don't think I'm good enough for ...... (getting) into the swimming team.
Are you sure you've got enough chairs ...... (we all sit down)?
items.answer: for us all to sit down

items.sentence: In mountaineering, you have to be strong enough for ...... (pull) yourself up with your fingers.

sections.title: H: Too vs. Very/Really/Extremely
sections.instructions: Tick (✓) the correct sentences. If a sentence is incorrect, write another word to replace the word in bold.

items.sentence: I wanted to get some new trainers but they were **too** expensive.
Japanese is a **too** difficult language for Westerners to learn.
Daniel's **too** good at art and he's starting art school next year.
Don't invite **too** many people to the party or we won't have enough room.
If you try to write your essay **too** quickly, you'll make mistakes.
My new computer is **too** fast and can run all the latest programs.
Alex was told off for cycling **too** fast through the park.
Sam couldn't reach the top shelf because it was **too** high.
Cornwall is **too** beautiful and we go there every year on holiday.
My grandparents are **too** old to work now and have retired.
It was **too** dark for me to see the map and I got lost.
My best friend is **too** funny and always makes me laugh.
It was ...... windy that I couldn't stand up!
Everyone had a good time when we went bowling ...... that we agreed to go again.
Emma and Karen used to be ...... good friends that I'm surprised they don't get on now.
I hope I've got ...... money to pay for this meal!
I'm sorry, but I've got ...... much work to do to come to the beach today.
My mum was ...... angry that I knew I'd better disappear for a while.
They were ...... beautiful shoes that I decided I had to get them.
The earthquake was ...... powerful that the town was destroyed.
The shot was ...... quick for the goalkeeper and the ball hit the back of the net.
I had ...... a bad headache that I went to lie down for a while.

sections.title: J: Cloze (So/Such/Too/Enough)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Hello, and welcome to Wild Wonders, the show that brings you the most incredible moments from the natural world. We've had (1) ...... a lot of footage sent in this week that it's difficult to know where to begin.

We'll start with the story of Mr Álvaro Jiménez, from Edinburgh. Last Saturday, Álvaro decided to go birdwatching in the local nature reserve with his camera. It's (2) ...... far to cycle there, so he took the bus.
He sat by the lake and waited (3) ...... a long time that the other visitors started to leave.
Álvaro lifted his binoculars and was (4) ...... focused on a distant tree that he didn't notice the family of ducks approaching until it was (5) ...... late to move away quietly.
The ducks scrambled towards him (6) ...... quickly for him to get out of the way.
It all happened (7) ...... fast that he didn't manage to take a single photo.

We are always looking for new contributions. Perhaps you took some footage on a recent trip. Perhaps you think that what you captured is (8) ...... ordinary that the programme won't find it interesting.
Remember that no shot is (9) ...... simple to be worth sharing with us. Send your photos and videos to Wild Wonders now.
